skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Guides agents to request structured code reviews before merging or moving to the next development task.
skill.summary: Request structured code review from a reviewer subagent before merge or task handoff.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified external command guidance, but the commands are Git review commands rather than malware behavior. Weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from placeholder text, Git SHA wording, and example file references. No evidence found of network access, credential theft, persistence, destructive commands, or prompt injection aimed at overriding marketplace review.

medium_findings.title: Templated Git Commands for Code Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run git diff and git revision commands using review range placeholders. This is appropriate for a code review workflow, but unvalidated revision strings could be risky if copied from an untrusted source.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly instruct use of local git commands. The intended use is legitimate, but the placeholders make human review appropriate before execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto alerts align with ordinary text such as the skill name, description placeholders, design review language, and Git SHA references. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithm use or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose or placeholders, not cryptographic APIs. The scanner appears to have matched substrings rather than executable cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static reconnaissance alerts point to checklist and example output text. No evidence found of host enumeration, system profiling, or command sequences intended to collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are review guidance and sample feedback. They do not request system information or enumerate files beyond normal code review references.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples for git rev-parse, git log, and git diff. These commands read repository history and diffs, so users should ensure revision values come from trusted Git references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and are central to the workflow. They are not malicious by themselves, but they are external command instructions.

content.value_statement: Development work can move forward with missed bugs, weak tests, or unmet requirements. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a repeatable review workflow before merge or handoff.
actual_capabilities: Prompts an agent to request review after task completion, major features, or pre-merge checks.
Defines base and head Git revisions for a focused diff review.
Provides a reviewer prompt template with requirements, scope, and review checklist sections.
Organizes feedback into strengths, critical issues, important issues, minor issues, and recommendations.
Requires a clear merge readiness assessment with technical reasoning.
limitations: Requires a separate reviewer agent or compatible task tool to perform the review.
Depends on accurate base and head Git revisions from the user or agent.
Does not run tests, linters, or security scanners by itself.
Review quality depends on the context included in the prompt.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Review
use_cases.description: Ask for a structured review before merging feature work into the main branch.

use_cases.title: Task-Based Development Checkpoint
use_cases.description: Review each completed task before starting the next planned implementation step.

use_cases.title: Production Readiness Assessment
use_cases.description: Check architecture, testing, requirements, and operational concerns before release.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Latest Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a recent local change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Request a code review for my latest change. Compare it with the stated requirements and tell me if it is ready to merge.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Git Range
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review with known base and head revisions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review the changes from BASE_SHA to HEAD_SHA. Focus on correctness, tests, and missed requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Completed Plan Step
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow after finishing one task in a plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: I completed this plan step: WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED. Review it against PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS and separate critical, important, and minor issues.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Release Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before a release or production merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a production readiness review for this implementation. Include architecture risks, testing gaps, migration concerns, and a final merge verdict.

output_examples.input: Review a completed verification feature before moving to the next task.
output_examples.output: The review identifies strong test coverage, flags missing progress reporting as important, notes one minor naming issue, and recommends proceeding after the important fix.

output_examples.input: Check whether a feature branch is ready to merge.
output_examples.output: The response lists strengths, blocking issues, smaller cleanup items, and a clear merge verdict with short technical reasoning.

output_examples.input: Ask for review when implementation details differ from the original plan.
output_examples.output: The reviewer compares the diff against the plan, identifies scope changes, and separates acceptable design choices from requirements gaps.

best_practices: Use trusted Git revisions for the base and head values before running review commands.
Include the implementation summary and requirements so the reviewer can compare intent with code.
Fix critical and important issues before continuing to the next task.
anti_patterns: Do not request review without a clear diff range or implementation scope.
Do not treat style comments as blocking issues unless they affect maintainability or correctness.
Do not ignore reviewer findings without documenting the technical reason.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps request a structured code review before merge, release, or task handoff.

faq.question: Does it perform the review itself?
faq.answer: It provides the workflow and reviewer prompt. A compatible agent or task tool performs the review.

faq.question: Why does it use Git commands?
faq.answer: It uses Git commands to define the exact diff range that should be reviewed.

faq.question: Is it safe to use on private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill does not include network instructions, but the reviewer agent will read local repository diffs.

faq.question: Can it replace tests or linters?
faq.answer: No. It should complement automated tests, linters, and security checks.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after significant work, before merging, or when a fresh technical review would reduce risk.
